violations.description: Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Fresh beef patties, cooked bacon, etc food items held in cold hold without proper date marking.--Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Ice cream from ice cream machine repackaged in containers were not date mark -best used by or packaged date - 10 calendar days.

Preventing contamination by employees. — --Employee observed handling ready-to-eat food with bare hands. Observed food employee holding gyro bread with bare hands while applying sauce. He also added cucumbers with his bare hands to the gyro. Ready-to-eat foods should be handled with gloved hands or utensils.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service may occur. Opened canned beverage was found on the prep table. Personal items should be stored separated from food items in facility.
